# `breeze pipeline run` drives every stage in order via the same RPCs a manual
# per-stage CLI call would use, instead of requiring one `stage start`/`approve`
# call per stage by hand. It stops (never auto-approves) at an approval stage,
# skips a stage that's already succeeded (so re-running after a manual approval
# continues from where it left off), and stops immediately on the first stage
# whose own outcome fails.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze identity register ci
cp stdout ci.token
exec breeze identity register alice
cp stdout alice.token
exec breeze role assign deployer ci --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze role assign reviewer alice --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

# A fanned-out pipeline requires --env up front — fails fast, before touching
# any stage, rather than getting partway through and only then discovering it
# can't proceed.
! exec breeze pipeline run release abc123 --as ci --token-file ci.token
stderr 'fans out'

# First run: build succeeds, then the runner stops at the approval stage
# instead of trying to approve it itself.
! exec breeze pipeline run release abc123 --env staging --as ci --token-file ci.token
stdout 'build: succeeded'
stdout 'review: awaiting approval \(0/1, role "reviewer"\)'
stdout 'breeze stage approve release review abc123'
! stdout 'deploy'

exec breeze stage approve release review abc123 --as alice --token-file alice.token

# Second run: build and review are both already succeeded, so they're skipped
# (not re-triggered) — only deploy and test actually run.
exec breeze pipeline run release abc123 --env staging --as ci --token-file ci.token
stdout 'build: succeeded \(already\)'
stdout 'review: succeeded \(already\)'
stdout 'deploy: succeeded'
stdout 'test: succeeded'
stdout 'pipeline "release" complete'

exec breeze pipeline status release abc123 --json
stdout '"stage":\s*"deploy"[\s\S]*"status":\s*"succeeded"'
stdout '"stage":\s*"test"[\s\S]*"status":\s*"succeeded"'

# A pipeline whose first stage fails outright: the runner stops there instead
# of trying anything further (there's nothing further to try in this pipeline,
# but the error still names exactly what stopped it).
exec breeze apply -f brittle.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token
! exec breeze pipeline run brittle def456 --as ci --token-file ci.token
stdout 'build: failed'
stderr 'stopped at "build"'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  environments = ["staging"]

  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/true", "{commit}"]
  }
  stage "review" {
    type               = "approval"
    required_approvals = 1
    approver_role      = "reviewer"
  }
  stage "deploy" {
    type          = "deploy"
    fans_out      = true
    required_role = "deployer"
    timeout       = "10s"
    command       = ["/bin/true", "{commit}", "{environment}"]
  }
  stage "test" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/true", "{environment}"]
  }
}

-- brittle.hcl --
pipeline "brittle" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/false"]
  }
}
